best_for: A single enforced convention: `signup_completed`, `checkout_started` — lowercase snake_case, past-tense verb, no per-page variants
avoid_when: Free-form names added ad hoc by whoever ships the feature
tradeoffs: Analyses stay queryable for years; requires one owner who reviews every new event name

best_for: Events defined in a typed module (one function per event, typed properties) reviewed via PR — `scripts/analytics/generate.py` scaffolds this
avoid_when: Letting every dev call `track('anything', {...})` with raw strings
tradeoffs: Typos become compile errors and the track plan is diffable; adds one small layer of indirection

best_for: Calling identify(userId) exactly at login/signup and reset() at logout; anonymous IDs merge into the user then
avoid_when: Identifying with email as the ID, or identifying on every page load
tradeoffs: Funnels survive the anonymous→known transition; getting merge behavior right per platform takes reading their docs once

best_for: Business-meaningful transitions: `subscription_upgraded`, not `upgrade_button_clicked` — clicks are implementation details
avoid_when: UI-coupled events that break with every redesign
tradeoffs: Events survive redesigns and map to revenue questions; needs a moment of modeling thought per feature

best_for: A shared property set (plan, role, source) attached via super-properties/context, not re-derived per event
avoid_when: Stuffing PII into properties because it's convenient (see AN021)
tradeoffs: Segmentation works uniformly across all events; standards doc must exist and be enforced in review

best_for: Define the product's activation moment first, then instrument the 3-6 steps leading to it
avoid_when: Instrumenting 40 steps and hoping insight emerges
tradeoffs: Every funnel step maps to a decision someone will actually make; requires agreeing what activation means

option: Cohort retention on the core action
best_for: Weekly cohorts retained on the action that defines product value (not mere logins)
avoid_when: Calendar-week vanity retention that mixes cohorts
tradeoffs: The one chart that says whether the product works; painful to admit when it's flat

best_for: A single metric the team optimizes (e.g. weekly active teams) with input metrics each squad can move
avoid_when: Dashboard sprawl where 40 charts each have zero owners
tradeoffs: Focus; picking the wrong north star misdirects quarters — revisit it explicitly

best_for: Emitting `order_completed`, `subscription_renewed` from the backend (webhook/DB layer), not the browser
avoid_when: Trusting client-side purchase events that ad blockers and closed tabs eat
tradeoffs: Revenue events become exact; client events remain for UX behavior — two streams, clearly named

best_for: A staging check that new events fire once, with expected properties, under consent-granted and denied states
avoid_when: Shipping instrumentation untested then discovering three weeks of silent nulls
tradeoffs: Data you can trust; one more checklist item per release (fold into the Testing module's flow)
